rulings.Case Title: UNITED STATES FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. SHEPPARD, MULLIN, RICHTER & HAMPTON, A LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Special Mtn To Strike Pursuant To Ccp 425.16
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, DECEMBER 4, 2007, LINE 11, DEFENDANT SHEPPARD, MULLIN, RICHTER & HAMPTON, A LIMITED'S Notice of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Pursuant To Ccp 425.16. The motion is denied. Based on Peregrine Funding Inc. v. Sheppard Mullin Richter & Hampton (2005) 133 Cal.App.4th 658, 671-673, the Court must conclude the complaint implicates protected activity given both the claim for relief and that the breach involves, in part, representing an adverse party in litigation. Having considered the evidence presented by US Fire, it has demonstrated a probability of prevailing. It is undisputed that Sheppard received confidential information when previously representing US Fire and that the party or parties whom Sheppard presently represents have interests adverse to US Fire. Sheppard contends that the matters are not substantially related. However, both companies - Kelly Moore and Plant - were/are deeply entangled in asbestos litigation and both companies wanted/want to establish coverage and to maximize recovery from insurance carriers. In Kelly Moore, Sheppard sought to protect US Fire and minimize its responsibility for the Kelly Moore's conduct in causing asbestos injuries. Here, Sheppard's representation is admittedly adverse and Sheppard is representing clients whose interests are to maximize the recovery from US Fire. Although it cannnot be said with certainty that Sheppard has breached its fiduciary duty to US Fire, that is not the test. It is enough that US Fire has established a legally sufficient claim. The evidentiary objections will be ruled on at the hearing. =(302/PJM/AY)
